mission.title: Install and Configure Kind (Kubernetes in Docker)
mission.description: Kind (Kubernetes in Docker) is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container nodes. It is primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself but can also be used for local development or CI. The key trade-off is that it runs entirely in Docker, which may not reflect production environments accurately.

steps.title: Install Kind
steps.description:
This step installs Kind using the pre-built binary for your operating system.

```bash

[ $(uname -m) = x86_64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

[ $(uname -m) = aarch64 ] && curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-arm64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> For AMD64 / x86_64 on Linux
> For ARM64 on Linux
> Make the Kind binary executable
> Move Kind to a directory in your PATH

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

```bash

kind create cluster --image kindest/node:v1.31.0

```

> Creates a cluster with Kubernetes version 1.31.0

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step sets up the kubeconfig file to interact with your new cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path)

```

> Sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the Kind cluster's kubeconfig

steps.title: Verify the Cluster
steps.description:
This step verifies that the cluster is running and nodes are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Should show the nodes in 'Ready' status

uninstall.title: Remove the Kind Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kind cluster and all associated resources. Note that this will delete all workloads and data within the cluster.

```bash

kind delete cluster

```

> Removes the Kind cluster

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kind
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step upgrades Kind to the latest version.

```bash

curl -Lo ./kind https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/dl/v0.31.0/kind-$(uname)-amd64

chmod +x ./kind

sudo mv ./kind /usr/local/bin/kind

```

> Download the latest Kind binary
> Make the new Kind binary executable
> Replace the old Kind binary with the new one

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kind: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind binary is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure that /usr/local/bin is in your PATH or move the Kind binary to a directory that is in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to start a Docker container'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker is not running or not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Start Docker and ensure it is running correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kind cluster may have failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kind get logs' and troubleshoot the issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure KUBECONFIG is set correctly with 'export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path)'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31.0, improved error handling.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required when upgrading from v0.30.0.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure Docker is installed and running on your machine.
